title: HMOR homing droid

{{Quote|A homing droid! It must have been planted on the ''Falcon'' back on the Death Star so they could follow us here!|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]|<ref name="Droids (1986) 8">[[Droids (1986) 8|''Droids'' (1986) 8]]</ref>}}
The '''HMOR homing droid''' was a [[Class five droid/Legends|fifth-degree]] [[homing droid]] produced by the [[Imperial Department of Military Research/Legends|Imperial Department of Military Research]] and used during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Design==
{{Quote|What?! It's also programmed to seek out and destroy this base's main power generator?!|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]|<ref name="Droids (1986) 8" />}}
[[File:HMORhomingdroid2-NEGTD.png|thumb|left|250px|An HMOR homing droid]]
The HMOR unit was larger than most homing [[Droid/Legends|droids]], and was considered to have an ill-tempered artificial personality. Equipped with both a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] and a set of wheels, the droid was able to infiltrate many high-security locations, even those where the presence of an unfamiliar repulsor field would trigger an alarm. The droid carried a detachable [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing device]] on its back, which it would leave behind to set pursuers off its trail. The HMOR's active signal was broadcast over hyperwave, and had a range of tens of thousands of [[Light-year/Legends|light-years]].<ref name="NEGD" />

The HMOR droid was programmed with an extensive database of over four-and-a-half million [[Starship/Legends|starship]] and [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] classifications, appropriated by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] scientists from the [[Pistoeka sabotage droid/Legends|Pistoeka sabotage droid]] once used by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="NEGD" />

If discovered, HMOR units were programmed to cause as much damage as possible before self-destructing to ensure any data it carried would not fall into enemy hands. To this end, each droid was equipped with two explosive [[defense drone]]s, and, alongside its manipulator arm, featured a retractable arm that could spray an acid stream at targets.<ref name="NEGD" />

==History==
{{Quote|You're sure the homing beacon is secure aboard their ship? I'm taking an awful risk, Vader. This had better work.|[[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]]|<ref name="Episode 4">{{Film|IV}}</ref>}}
HMOR droids were used as tracking devices by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Imperial personnel secreted a HMOR homing droid aboard [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] while it was held aboard the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. The vessel escaped the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] and rendezvoused with [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]—the presence of the HMOR droid thus ensuring the Imperials now had precise coordinates of the secret [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] base. The droid was discovered on Yavin 4 by [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], and launched its seeker drones in retaliation. It then attempted to destroy [[Great Temple/Legends|Massassi Station's]] [[Power generator/Legends|power generator]], but was thwarted by C-3PO who trapped it between two closing [[Blast door/Legends|blast doors]], slicing the droid in half.

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Homing_droid.png|thumb|right|200px|The homing droid as depicted in [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']]]]
{{Quote|As you can see, the original droid was very cartoony, and Ian Fullwood did a great job at redesigning it so it looks SW-real.|[[Daniel Wallace]]|<ref name="Endnotes">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2006/07/endnotes-for-new-essential-guide-to.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for The New Essential Guide to Droids: PART 3 OF 3}}</ref>}}
The HMOR homing droid first appeared in [[Star Comics]]' [[Droids (1986) 8|''Droids'' (1986) 8]]. It went unmentioned again until it received an entry in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''. Author [[Daniel Wallace]] included the droid at the suggestion of [[Abel G. Peña]], who also supplied Wallace with material from the original comic for reference. Artist [[Ian Fullwood]] redesigned the cartoonish homing droid depicted in the comic to appear realistic in the context of ''Star Wars''; he also incorporated into the overall design the homing beacon shown in [[Empire 15|''Empire'' 15]] to show that the homing droid carried and planted the beacon seen in that comic.<ref name="Endnotes" />
